operating_state: Total revenues increased to $6.0 billion driven by rental revenue growth from positive rent change on lease rollovers and same-store NOI growth, with consolidated operating portfolio occupancy at 96.2%. Real Estate Segment NOI increased driven by rental rate growth, acquisitions, and development completions, while Strategic Capital Segment NOI decreased due to lower promote revenues. Operating distributions from unconsolidated entities totaled $417 million versus $536 million in prior year.
cost_structure: Rental expenses increased to $1.3 billion driven by portfolio growth and inflationary pressures. General and administrative expenses rose due to inflationary increases and higher compensation expenses, partially offset by capitalized G&A costs. Cash paid for interest net of capitalized amounts rose significantly to $573 million from $340 million reflecting higher borrowing costs.
material_events: Generated $2.4 billion in net proceeds from property contributions to co-investment ventures and third-party sales. Completed $5.3 billion total debt issuance including senior notes with 4.8% weighted average interest rate and 14-year weighted average maturity. Established commercial paper program for short-term funding flexibility and acquired land in India through consolidated joint ventures.
macro_exposures: Experiencing customer caution in leasing decisions due to uncertainty in economic and geopolitical environment, with slower decision-making on build-to-suits expected to limit speculative development. Foreign currency exposure creates ongoing volatility, partially mitigated through natural hedging via borrowing in functional currencies and derivative instruments. Unconsolidated co-investment ventures carry third-party debt with 6-year weighted average maturity.
forward_outlook: Management expects customers to exercise caution through remainder of 2024 but believes company is well-positioned for long-term organic revenue growth given cumulative market rent increases and existing high lease mark-to-market of approximately 34%. Development pipeline includes properties that are 32.8% leased. Land bank and redevelopment sites support potential future development opportunities, while quarterly common stock dividends increased to $0.96 per share.
uncertainty_profile: Consolidated development portfolio investment requires additional funding to complete, with leasing success dependent on market conditions. Debt maturities require refinancing or repayment, while equity commitments to co-investment ventures may require property contributions or cash funding through 2033. REIT status maintenance requires meeting dividend distribution requirements while retaining sufficient cash for capital deployment.
